To initiate the movement, exhale and pull the back of your hand up towards the ceiling. Keep you arm at full reach (elbow slightly bent) as you go and continue until you get to shoulder height.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the dumbbell along the same path back into its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variations: Front Cable Raise or Kettlebell Arnold Press.
Lie down on a flat surface with your back pressing against the floor or an exercise mat.  Make sure your knees are bent.

Have a partner hand you the bar on one hand.  When starting, your arm should be just about fully extended, similar to the starting position of a barbell bench press.  However, this time your grip will be neutral (palms facing your torso).
Make sure the hand you are not using to lift the weight is placed by your side.
Begin the exercise by lowering the barbell until your elbow touches the ground.  Make sure to breathe in as this is the eccentric (lowering part of the exercise).
Then start lifting the barbell back up to the original starting position.  Remember to breathe out during the concentric (lifting part of the exercise).
Repeat until you have performed your recommended repetitions.
Switch arms and repeat the movement.
Variations: You can also perform this exercise using dumbbells.  By using dumbbells you will be able to isolate your chest and add some variety to your workouts.

Caution: When you are done, do not drop the barbell or dumbbell next to you as this is dangerous to your rotator cuff in your shoulders and others working out around you.  A better way to do this is to use both of your hands to grab the barbell or dumbbell and place it to your side.  Simply move the barbell or dumbbell away from the arm you are exercising before dropping the weights.  This will ensure optimal safety and prevent any injuries.

Grab a dumbbell in your right hand and take a seat on a flat bench. Lay down and bring the weight beside your chest. Press the dumbbell towards the ceiling with an underhand grip (palm facing your head) until you have nearly locked out your elbow, a slight bend should be present to preserve your joint. With your left arm you should be supporting your right elbow. This will be your starting position.

Maintaining this slight flex at your elbow, inhale and bend at your right elbow creating an arcing motion with the dumbbell so that your hand is heading towards your forehead. The weight should be stretching your triceps. Remember your upper arm shouldn't move throughout.
Once the dumbbell is just off your forehead pause for a brief moment, exhale and return the dumbbell and your hand along the same path to the starting position.
Pause for a second and repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions before swapping sides.
Variations: Tricep Extension (Rope) or Bench Dips.
Grab a dumbbell in each hand and adopt an upright, standing position. Have your arms at full extension, relaxed by your sides.
Next, whilst maintaining a slight bend at your elbows, exhale and lift the dumbbells out in front of you until they are at shoulder height.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the weights to your side.
Again, exhale but this time lift the dumbbells out laterally to your sides, pause when you get to shoulder level.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the weights to your side.
Continue to alternate in this fashion until you have completed the prescribed amount of repetitions.
Variations: Seated Arnold Press or Bradford:Rocky Presses.
Grab a dumbbell in each hand and adopt a standing shoulder width stance. Bring the dumbbells in towards your chest with your palms facing your torso. This is your starting position.

Exhale and press one of the dumbbells straight up and above your head towards the ceiling, rotating your wrist as you go. Your palm should now be facing forwards with your arm at full extension (keep a slight bend at your elbow). As you press the dumbbell, lean slightly towards the opposite side to enhance the 'see-saw' action.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the dumbbell along the same path back into its starting position.
Alternate as you go and complete the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Arnold Dumbbell Press or Kettlebell Arnold Press.
Grab a couple of dumbbells and take a seat on a bench. Have your arms by your side at full extension, palms facing each other. This will be your starting position.
Whilst maintaining a stationary torso, elbows slightly bent and exhaling throughout, lift the dumbbells out to your sides (on a wide arc) until they are parallel with the floor.
After a brief pause at the top, return the dumbbells to the starting position along the same path.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Standing Dumbbell Lat Raises or Cable Lateral Raises.

name_en: Seated Single Arm Wrist Curls Palm Up
instructions_en: Grab a dumbbell in your right hand and take a seat on a bench just off vertical. Adopt an underhand grip (palm facing the ceiling), with your knees bent at a 90 degree angle. Next you will need to place the back of your wrist on the end of your right knee so that the dumbbell is not in contact with your body and relaxed towards to floor. This will be your starting position.
To initiate the movement, exhale and curl the dumbbell upwards so that you are curling your palm towards your torso. Keep your wrist in contact with your knee throughout the movement.
Continue on an upward motion until your forearm is optimally contracted.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the dumbbell along the same path back to its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variation: Palms-Up Barbell Wrist Curls Over A Bench.

name_en: Seated Single Arm Wrist Curls Palm Down
instructions_en: Grab a dumbbell in your right hand and take a seat on a bench just off vertical. Adopt an overhand grip (palm facing the floor), with your knees bent at a 90 degree angle. Next you will need to place your wrist on the end of your right knee so that the dumbbell is not in contact with your body and relaxed towards to floor. This will be your starting position.
To initiate the movement, exhale and curl the dumbbell upwards so that you are curling the back of your hand towards your torso. Keep your wrist in contact with your knee throughout the movement.
Continue on an upward motion until your forearm is optimally contracted.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the dumbbell along the same path back to its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variation: Seated Palms-Down Barbell Wrist Curls.

Grab a dumbbell in each hand and adopt a shoulder width stance. Without leaning backward or putting your posture into jeopardy, press the dumbbells above your head so that you arms are fully extended, yet slightly flexed/bent at your elbows. Make sure your palms are facing forward at this point. This will be your starting position.

Slowly and in a controlled fashion, descend the dumbbells until they reach the side of your head (parallel with your ears), creating a 90 degree angle at your elbows. Inhale on the downward motion and flare your elbows out wide.
After a brief pause, exhale and press the dumbbells back along this path to their starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Seated Dumbbell Shoulder Press or Cable Shoulder Press.

Grab a dumbbell in each hand and take a seat on a bench just off vertical. Adopt an underhand grip (palms facing the ceiling), with your knees bent at a 90 degree angle. Next you will need to place the back of your wrists on the ends of your knees so that the dumbbells are not in contact with your body and relaxed towards to floor. This will be your starting position.
To initiate the movement, exhale and curl the dumbbells upwards so that you are curling your palms towards your torso. Keep your wrists in contact with your knees throughout the movement.
Continue on an upward motion until your forearms are optimally contracted.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the dumbbells along the same path back to their starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Palms-Up Barbell Wrist Curls Over A Bench.

Grab a dumbbell in each hand and take a seat on a bench just off vertical. Adopt an overhand grip (palms facing the floor), with your knees bent at a 90 degree angle. Next you will need to place your wrists on the ends of your knees so that the dumbbells are not in contact with your body and relaxed towards to floor. This will be your starting position.
To initiate the movement, exhale and curl the dumbbells upwards so that you are pulling the back of your hands towards your torso. Keep your wrists in contact with your knees throughout the movement.
Continue on an upward motion until your forearms are optimally contracted.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the dumbbells along the same path back to their starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Seated Palms-Down Barbell Wrist Curls.

Grab a dumbbell in each hand and take a seat on a bench just off vertical. Allow your arms to hang perpendicular to the floor, by your sides with an underhand grip (palms facing away from you). This will be your starting position.
To initiate the movement, exhale and curl the dumbbells both up and outward whilst maintaining your underhand grip.
Continue on an upward motion until your biceps are optimally contracted.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the dumbbells along the same path back to their starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Wide-Grip Standing Barbell Curls or Concentration Curls.

Grab a dumbbell in each hand and take a seat on the end of a bench. Whilst maintaining a straight back, you should lean forward from your hips until your torso is almost parallel with the floor. Have your knees bent at 90 degrees and your elbows pinned in to the side of your torso. Your elbows should be at 90 degrees at this point. This is your starting position.

Whilst keeping your upper arms stationary, use your triceps to lift the dumbbells on an arcing motion behind you. Exhale as you do so and continue to lift the dumbbells until your forearms are parallel to the floor and your elbows are fully extended. Tip: Only your forearms should move.
After a brief pause, slowly lower the dumbbells along the same path back into their starting position as you inhale.
Repeat the movement for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Tricep Extensions (Rope) or Dips - Triceps Version.

Grab a couple of dumbbells and lay face down on an incline bench (around 30-45 degrees) and allow your arms and shoulders to relax and hang perpendicular to the floor with a neutral grip (palms facing each other). Have your head off the end of the bench so that only your torso is supported. This will be your starting position.
Whilst exhaling, contract the muscles across the top of your back and pull your shoulder blades together (retracting your shoulders).
After a brief pause, inhale as you lower the dumbbells along the same path towards the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Barbell Row or Cable Row.

Grab a weight plate with an overhand pinched grip (palms facing your body), and allow your arms the hang perpendicular to the floor in front of you. Adopt a standing position with a shoulder width stance and a straight back. Keep your elbows pinned in close to your torso for the duration of the exercise. This will be your starting position.
Whilst keeping your upper arms stationary, curl the weight plate upward toward your shoulders by flexing your biceps. Exhale as you do so and remember, only your forearms should move.
Continue in this fashion until your biceps are fully flexed and the weight plate is as high as it can be.
Pause for a brief moment and then return the plate back along the same path toward the starting position as you inhale.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Reverse Barbell Preacher Curls or Reverse Cable Curls.

name_en: Seated Dumbbell Rear Delt Flyes Overhand Grip
instructions_en: To begin, lie down on an incline bench set at a 30-degree angle with the chest and stomach pressing against the incline.

Have the dumbbells in each hand with the palms facing down to the floor.  Your arms should be in front of you so that they are perpendicular to the angle of the bench. Tip: Your elbows should have a slight bend.  The legs should be stationary while applying pressure with the ball of your toes (your heels should not be touching the floor).  This is the starting position.
Maintaining the slight bend of the elbows, move the weights out and away from each other in an arc motion while exhaling.

As you lift the weight, your wrist should externally rotate by 90-degrees so that you go from a palms down (pronated) grip to a palms facing each other (neutral) grip. Tip: Try to squeeze your shoulder blades together to get the best results from this exercise.
The arms should be elevated until they are level with the head.
Feel the contraction and slowly lower the weights back down to the starting position while inhaling.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

Grab two dumbbells and take a seat at the end of a bench. Lean forward from your waist whilst maintaining you posture. Lean as far as you can and allow the dumbbells to take their position behind your calves. At this point, your chest should be close to your knees and your palms should be facing one another. This will be your starting position.

Whilst maintaining good posture, bring the dumbbells out on a wide arc until your arms are parallel to the floor. Ensure you have a slight bend at the elbows and you are exhaling throughout this portion of the movement.
After a brief pause, slowly descend the dumbbells along the same path to their starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Cable Rear Delt Flyes or Rear Delt Flye Machine.

Grab a dumbbell by one of its ends in both hands and stand up straight. Maintain this straight posture throughout the movement and your arms remain passive and extended. Adopt a wide stance with your toes slightly facing outwards. This will be your starting position.

Inhale as you descend by bending at your knees. Remember to keep your knees behind your toes and put most of your weight through your heels. The dumbbell should now be just off the floor with your arms being between your legs.
After a brief pause, Push through your heels and thighs to bring your torso back up into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Make sure to maintain posture as failure to do so could result in injury.
Variations: Wide Stance Leg Press or Kettlebell Sumo Squats.
Stand two weight plates up beside one another on the floor next to you. Adopt a standing shoulder width stance. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, bend down (remember to bend your knees and lift through your legs) to pick up the weight plates by pinching them together. Do not use the handles, pinch the plate themselves between your fingers and thumb. Return to your standing position and pinch the plates firmly for the recommended amount of time before returning them to the floor. Swap side and repeat the process.

name_en: Wrist Curls Over A Bench Palms Up
instructions_en: Grab a two dumbbells and kneel down next to a bench. Lean over the bench so that your forearms are supported with your wrists over hanging (dumbbells in hand). Adopt a 'palms up' grip. This will be your starting position. Tip: Only your wrists should move when performing this exercise.
Exhale and curl your palms in towards your forearms.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your wrists along the same path to the starting position.
Repeat the movement for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Palms Up Barbell Wrist Curls.

name_en: Wrist Curls Over A Bench Palms Down
instructions_en: Grab two dumbbells and kneel down next to a bench. Lean over the bench so that your forearms are supported with your wrists over hanging (dumbbells in hand). Adopt an overhand 'palms facing down' grip. This will be your starting position. Tip: Only your wrists should move when performing this exercise.
Exhale and curl the back of your hands up towards your forearms.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your hands along the same path to the starting position.
Repeat the movement for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Palms Up Barbell Wrist Curls or Palms Up Dumbbell Wrist Curls.

name_en: One Arm Supinated Dumbbell Triceps Extension
instructions_en: Grab a dumbbell in your right hand and take a seat on a flat bench. Lay down and bring the weight beside your chest. Press the dumbbell towards the ceiling with an underhand grip (palm facing your head) until you have nearly locked out your elbow, a slight bend should be present to preserve your joint. With your left arm you should be supporting your right elbow. This will be your starting position.

Maintaining this slight flex at your elbow, inhale and bend at your right elbow creating an arcing motion with the dumbbell so that your hand is heading towards your forehead. The weight should be stretching your triceps. Remember your upper arm shouldn't move throughout.
Once the dumbbell is just off your forehead pause for a brief moment, exhale and return the dumbbell and your hand along the same path to the starting position.
Pause for a second and repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions before swapping sides.
Variations: Overhead Tricep Rope Extension or Bench Dips.

Grab a dumbbell in your right hand and take a seat on a flat bench. Lay down and bring the weight beside your chest. Press the dumbbell towards the ceiling with an underhand grip (palm facing your head) until you have nearly locked out your elbow, a slight bend should be present to preserve your joint. With your left arm you should be supporting your right elbow. This will be your starting position.

Maintaining this slight flex at your elbow, inhale and bend at your right elbow creating an arcing motion with the dumbbell so that your hand is heading towards your forehead. The weight should be stretching your triceps. Remember your upper arm shouldn't move throughout.
Once the dumbbell is just off your forehead pause for a brief moment, exhale and return the dumbbell and your hand along the same path to the starting position.
Pause for a second and repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions before swapping sides.
Variations: Tricep Extension (Rope) or Bench Dips.

Grab a dumbbell in your right hand and take a seat on a preacher curl machine. Place your right upper arm over the pad of the machine with your palm facing your shoulder. Start With the bicep fully contracted and the dumbbell close to your shoulder. This will be your starting position.
As you inhale, slowly lower the dumbbell until your upper arm is extended and your bicep is fully stretched.
Exhale and contract your bicep to lift the dumbbell up to the starting position.
Squeeze your bicep for a second at the contracted position and repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Switch hands and repeat the movement with your left arm.
Variations: Single Arm Cable Curl or Neutral Grip Preacher Curl.

name_en: Single Arm Lateral Raise Against Bench
instructions_en: Grab a dumbbell in your left hand and lay on your right side on an incline bench (around 30-45 degrees). Have your left arm extended (with a slight bend at your elbow) and allow the dumbbell to rest of your left thigh as you adopt an overhand grip (palm facing the floor). This will be your starting position.
To initiate the movement, exhale and elevate your left arm towards the ceiling. Keep lifting your arm and the dumbbell until the muscles in your shoulder are at peak contraction.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the dumbbell along the same path back to its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variations: Single Arm Lateral Raise or Cable Lateral Raise.

Grab a dumbbell in your right hand and take a seat on a flat bench. Lay down and bring the weight beside your chest. Press the dumbbell towards the ceiling with a neutral grip (palm facing inwards towards your body) until you have nearly locked out your elbow, a slight bend should be present to preserve your joint. Place your left arm across your torso for stability. This will be your starting position.
Maintaining this slight flex at your elbow, lower your arm in a wide arcing motion until you feel your chest stretching, inhale as you do so. Your arm should be parallel with the floor at this point.
Following the same path as you went down, squeeze your pectoral muscles and bring your arm back to the starting position, remembering to exhale as you go.
Pause for a second and then repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variations: Cable Crossover or Pec Deck.
Lie down on a flat bench with a dumbbell in one hand on top of your thigh.

By using your thigh to help you get the dumbbell up, clean the dumbbell up so that you can hold it in front of you at shoulder width.  Use the hand you are not lifting with to help position the dumbbell over you properly.
Once at shoulder width, rotate your wrist forward so that the palm of your hand is facing away from you.  This will be your starting position.

Bring down the weights slowly to your side as you breathe in.  Keep full control of the dumbbell at all times.  Tip: Use the hand that you are not lifting with to help keep the dumbbell balance as you may struggle a bit at first.  Only use your non-lifting hand if it is needed.  Otherwise, keep it resting to the side.

As you breathe out, push the dumbbells up using your pectoral muscles.  Lock your arms in the contracted position, squeeze your chest, hold for a second and then start coming down slowly.  Tip: It should take at least twice as long to go down than to come up.
Repeat the movement for the prescribed amount of repetitions of your training program.
Switch arms and repeat the movement.
Caution: When you are done, do not drop the dumbbell next to you as this is dangerous to your rotator cuff in your shoulders and others working out around you.

Just lift your legs from the floor bending at the knees, twist your wrist so that you can place the dumbbell on top of your thigh.  When the dumbbell is touching your thigh simultaneously push your upper torso up (while pressing the dumbbell on your thigh) and also perform a slight kick forward with your legs (keeping the dumbbell on top of the thigh).  By doing this combined movement, momentum will help you get back to a sitting position with the dumbbell still on top of your thigh.  At this moment you can place the dumbbell on the floor.

Grab a dumbbell and adopt a shoulder width stance. Bend your knees slightly and lean forward from your hips, maintaining a straight back. Continue to lean until your torso is just off parallel to the floor with your free hand leaning on a bench next to you. With the arm that holds the dumbbell, your elbow should be pinned close to your body with your forearm perpendicular to the floor. Tip: There should be a 90-degree angle between your forearm and your upper arms. This is your starting position.
Keeping your upper arm close to your body and with a neutral grip, lift your forearm behind you until the elbows are fully extended. Exhale as you lift the dumbbell and squeeze your tricep as you go.
After a brief pause at the top of the contraction, slowly lower the dumbbell along the same path until it is back to the starting position. Inhale as you go.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Rope Tricep Extension or Skull Crushers

name_en: Single Arm Dumbbell Hammer Preacher Curls
instructions_en: Grab a dumbbell in your right hand and take a seat on a preacher curl machine. Place your right upper arm over the pad of the machine with your palm facing inwards (the dumbbell should be perpendicular to the floor). Start With the bicep fully contracted and the dumbbell close to your shoulder. This will be your starting position.
As you inhale, slowly lower the dumbbell until your upper arm is extended and your bicep is fully stretched.
Exhale and contract your bicep to lift the dumbbell up to the starting position.
Squeeze your bicep for a second at the contracted position and repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Switch hands and repeat the movement with your left arm.
Variations: Single Arm Cable Curl or EZ Bar Preacher Curl.
Grab a dumbbell in each hand and adopt a standing position with your elbows tucked in, palms facing your torso. This will be your starting position.

Whilst maintaining a stationary upper arm, curl the left dumbbell up towards your shoulder by contracting your bicep. Keep your elbow pinned to your side and exhale as you do so. Squeeze your bicep for a brief moment at the contracted position. Tip: Only the forearm should have moved.
After this brief pause, inhale and return the dumbbell to the starting posiiton along the same path.
Repeat the movement with your right hand.
Continue alternating for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Seated Hammer Curls or Seated Dumbbell Curls.

Grab a couple of dumbbells and lay face down on an incline bench (around 30-45 degrees) and allow your arms and shoulders to relax and hang perpendicular to the floor with a neutral grip (palms facing each other). Have your head off the end of the bench so that only your torso is supported. This will be your starting position.
Whilst exhaling, contract the muscles across the top of your back and pull your shoulder blades together (retracting your shoulders).
After a brief pause, inhale as you lower the dumbbells along the same path towards the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Barbell Row or Cable Row.

Grab a dumbbell in each hand and have an underhand grip (palms facing up). Next, lay down on a flat bench and allow your arms to hang beside you, as close to vertical from the floor as possible. This will be your starting position.
Whilst exhaling, contract your biceps and curl the dumbbells up to shoulder height. Hold the contraction for a second as you squeeze your biceps. Tip: Only the forearms should move.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the dumbbells along the same path back into their starting position.
Repeat the movement for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Bicep Curls or Cable Curls.

Grab a dumbbell in your left hand and lay face down on an incline bench (around 30-45 degrees) and allow your left arm to hang perpendicular to the floor. Have your head off the end of the bench so that only your torso is supported with your right arm, placed behind your back. This will be your starting position.
Inhale and pull the back of your left hand towards the ceiling.
Once the dumbbell is horizontal to the floor, pause for a brief second, exhale and slowly return the dumbbell along the same path, back into its starting position.
Repeat until you have completed the prescribed amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variations: Face Pulls or Rear Delt Machine Flyes.

Grab a dumbbell in your left hand and lay on a bench (on your right side). Lay with your left arm across your body, allowing the dumbbell to lay next to your right shoulder, palm facing your torso. This is your starting position.
Inhale and pull the back of your left hand towards the wall behind you, therefore opening up your torso.
Once the dumbbell is vertical from the floor, pause for a brief second, exhale and slowly return the dumbbell along the same path, back into its starting position.
Repeat until you have completed the prescribed amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variations: Face Pulls or Rear Delt Machine Flyes.

name_en: Lying Face Up Plate Neck Resistance
instructions_en: Grab a weight plate and lay (face up) on a flat bench. Position your body so that your torso is supported by the bench but your head and neck are off the end. Gently, place the weight plate on your forehead, keeping hold of it for stability. Relax your neck so that your forehead is tilted back towards the floor. This is your starting position.
To initiate this movement, exhale and use the muscles in your neck to pull your head upward and attempt to get your chin on your chest.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your head along the same path back into its starting position.
Repeat the movement for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Be very cautious with the weight and avoid jerking motions at all costs!

name_en: Lying Face Down Plate Neck Resistance
instructions_en: Grab a weight plate and lay (face down) on a flat bench. Position your body so that your torso is supported by the bench but your head and neck are off the end. Gently, place the weight plate on the back of your head, keeping hold of it for stability. Relax your neck so that your forehead is tilted towards the floor. This is your starting position.
To initiate this movement, exhale and use the muscles in your neck to pull your head upward so you are looking forwards.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your head along the same path back into its starting position.
Repeat the movement for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Be very cautious with the weight and avoid jerking motions at all costs!

Grab two dumbbells and lay flat on a bench with the dumbbells pressed straight up towards the ceiling. Adopt a pronated grip (palms facing away from you), with the ends of the dumbbells gently touching one another and your elbows tucked in. This will be your starting position.

Inhale as you descend the dumbbells down towards your forehead. Be sure to keep your upper arms stationary and your elbows tucked in. When you get to within an inch of your forehead pause for a second.
Then whilst exhaling, use your triceps to return the dumbbells along the same path to the starting position. Your arms should be fully extended once again and pointing towards the ceiling.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: EZ-Bar Skullcrushers or Tricep Rope Extensions.
Grab a dumbbell with your left hand and adopt a standing position with your arms at full extension, palm facing your thigh. This will be your starting position.

Whilst maintaining a stationary position, lift the dumbbell on a wide arc away from your body until your arm becomes parallel with the floor. Have a slight bend at your elbow and exhale as you lift the weight.
As you inhale, return the dumbbell along the same path to it's starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap to your right side.
Variations: Lateral Raises (Double) or Seated Lateral Raises
Grab a dumbbell in each hand, adopt and shoulder width stance with your arms out wide, at shoulder height (replicating a 'T' shape).
Next, inhale and bend at your hips and knees and descend into a squat position. Simultaneously, keeping your arms straight, bring the dumbbells in towards each other to meet in front of your body.

After a brief pause, exhale and press through your heels to extend at your knees and hips to bring yourself back up to a standing position. At the same time, retract your shoulder blades and return the dumbbells out wide, keeping your arms at full length.
Repeat for the prescribed amount of repetitions.
Variation: Cable Iron Cross.
Grab two dumbbells and take a seat on an incline bench (around 45 degrees) with your arms by your side.
The palms of your hands should be facing your torso. This will be your starting position.

Whilst holding the upper arm stationary, exhale as you curl both dumbbells forward, contracting your biceps. Continue the movement until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level. Hold the contracted position for a second as you squeeze your biceps.
(Tip: Only the forearms should move.)
Inhale as you slowly begin to bring the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Standing Hammer Curls or Cable Curls.

Grab a dumbbell in each hand and lay down on an incline bench (around 30 degrees), bringing the dumbbells beside your chest. Keep your elbows at a 90-degree angle and your forearms perpendicular to the floor. Once here, keep your palms facing away from you, exhale and press the dumbbells towards the ceiling.
Squeeze your pectoral muscles when your arms are at full extension. Once you have completed this, inhale and bring your arms back down beside your chest until your elbows are at 90-degrees once more.
Repeat the movement for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Always have a spotter with you to help you throughout the movement and to help unload the dumbbells after completion.
Variations: Incline Barbell Bench Press or Smith Machine Incline Bench Press

name_en: Incline Dumbbell Flyes With A Twist
instructions_en: Grab two dumbbells and lie on an incline bench (chest facing the ceiling) that is set to an incline angle of around 30 degrees.
With a slight bend at the elbows, extend your arms towards the ceiling until they are nearly locked out.
Rotate your wrists so that your palms are now facing one another. This will be your starting position.

As you inhale, Lower your arms down on a wide arc as you rotate your wrists until you feel a stretch across your chest and the dumbbells are perpendicular to the floor. Tip: Keep your elbows slightly bent throughout the movement to reduce stress on your joints.

Once you have reached the bottom of the movement, pause for a brief moment before exhaling and returning the dumbbells (along the same path) to their starting position, above your chest. At this point your arms should still be extended and the motion is controlled through your pectoral muscles.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Incline Dumbbell Flyes or Pec Deck
Grab two dumbbells and lie on an incline bench (chest facing the ceiling) that is set to an incline angle of around 30 degrees.
With a slight bend at the elbows, extend your arms until they are nearly locked out, towards the ceiling.
Rotate your wrists so that your palms are now facing one another. This will be your starting position.
As you inhale, Lower your arms down on a wide arc until you feel a stretch across your chest. Tip: Keep your elbows slightly bent throughout the movement to reduce stress on your joints.

Once you have reached the bottom of the movement, pause for a brief moment before exhaling and returning the dumbbells (along the same path) to their starting position, above your chest. At this point your arms should still be extended and the motion is controlled through your pectoral muscles.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Incline Flyes With A Twist or Pec Deck

name_en: Neutral Grip Incline Dumbbell Chest Press
instructions_en: Grab a dumbbell in each hand and lay down on an incline bench (around 30 degrees), bringing the dumbbells beside your chest. Keep your elbows at a 90-degree angle and your forearms perpendicular to the floor. Once here, bring your thumb the same side of the dumbbell as your fingers. keep your palms facing away from you.
To initiate this movement, exhale and press the dumbbells towards the ceiling. Squeeze your pectoral muscles as you go and continue to press until your arms are at full extension.
After a brief pause, inhale and bring your arms back down beside your chest until your elbows are at 90 degrees once more.
Repeat the movement for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Always have a spotter with you (especially with this grip) to help you throughout the movement and to help unload the dumbbells after completion.
Variations: Incline Barbell Bench Press or Smith Machine Incline Bench Press.
Grab a dumbbell and hold it by one of it's ends, close to your chest. Adopt a standing position with a shoulder width stance. This will be your starting position.
As you inhale, bend at your knees and waist in order to squat until your thighs become parallel (or just past) to the floor. Tip: Maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
At the bottom of the movement, pause for a moment, then push through your heels whilst exhaling until you return to a standing position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: If you have any lower back issues, this may not be the most suitable exercise for you. A suggestion would be to try a Leg Press Machine instead.
Variation: Kettlebell Goblet Squat or Wide Stance Leg Press.
Grab a barbell and lay down on a flat bench. Adopt an overhand grip (palms facing your thighs) and at full arms reach, allow the barbell to rest across your thighs. This is your starting position.

Inhale and pull the bar in a semicircular motion up and over your torso, maintaining a slight bend at your elbows as you go. Continue to extend the barbell up and over your body until your arms are parallel with the floor above your head. Your palms will then be facing the ceiling.
After a brief pause, exhale and return the barbell back along the same path to its starting position.
Repeat until you have completed the prescribed amount of repetitions.
Variations: Arnold Dumbbell Press or Cable Front Raise.

Grab a weight plate and adopt a shoulder width, standing stance. Next, make sure that you grip the plate in a '9' and '3' O'Clock position. Allow your arms to hang in front of your torso, vertical from the floor. This will be your starting position.
To initiate the movement, exhale and in an arcing motion, lift the weight plate up in front of your body until you reach head height.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the weight plate back along the same path to its starting position.
Repeat until you have completed the prescribed amount of repetitions.
Variations: Arnold Dumbbell Press or Cable Front Raise.

Grab a dumbbell in each hand and lay on an incline bench (around 45-60 degrees). Have your arms by your sides at full extension, with a neutral grip (palms facing each other). This will be your starting position.

Whilst maintaining a slight bend at your elbows, exhale and lift the dumbbells in an arcing motion up in front of you, rotating your wrists as you go until you get to head height. You should now have an overhand grip (palms facing the floor).
After a brief pause, inhale and return the dumbbells along the same path back to their starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Front Cable Raise or Alternating Front Raises.

Pick a couple of dumbbells and stand with a straight torso and the dumbbells on front of your thighs at arms length with the palms of the hand facing your thighs.  This will be your starting position.

While maintaining the torso stationary (no swinging), lift the left dumbbell to the front with a slight bend on the elbow and the palms of the hands always facing down.  Continue to go up until you arm is slightly above parallel to the floor.  Exhale as you execute this portion of the movement and pause for a second at the top. Inhale after the second pause.
Now lower the dumbbell back down slowly to the starting position as you simultaneously lift the right dumbbell.
Continue alternating in this fashion until all of the recommended amount of repetitions have been performed for each arm.
Variations: This exercise can also be performed both arms at the same time.  Also, you could use a barbell as well.
Grab a dumbbell in each hand and adopt an upright, standing position. Have your arms at full extension, relaxed by your sides.
Next, whilst maintaining a slight bend at your elbows, exhale and lift the dumbbells out in front of you until they are at shoulder height.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the weights to your side.
Again, exhale but this time lift the dumbbells out laterally to your sides, pause when you get to shoulder level.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the weights to your side.
Continue to alternate in this fashion until you have completed the prescribed amount of repetitions.
Variations: Seated Arnold Press or Bradford:Rocky Presses.
Grab a dumbbell in each hand and take a seat on an incline bench. Have your arms by your side at full extension, palms facing each other. This will be your starting position.

Whilst maintaining a stationary upper arm and elbows tucked in close to your torso, curl the dumbbells up towards your shoulders. As you go, exhale and rotate your palms so that they are facing yourself. Squeeze your biceps for a brief moment once they are fully contracted before descending the dumbbells along the same path as you inhale (Remember to rotate your wrists as you bring the dumbbells back to their starting position).
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Close-Grip EZ Bar Curl or Cable Curls.

Grab two dumbbells with an overhand grip. The ends of the dumbbells gently touching one another as they rest on your thighs, arms hanging perpendicular to the floor. Tip: Maintain a straight back and keep the dumbbells as close to your body as possible throughout the movement.

Exhale and pull the dumbbells up towards your chin by contracting your shoulders and flaring your elbows out wide. Continue to pull them upward until you reach your chin. At this point your elbows should be above the dumbbells.
Pause for a brief moment, then inhale and return the dumbbells along the same path towards the starting position.
Variations: Barbell Upright Rows or Smith Machine Upright Row
Lie down on a flat bench holding two dumbbells directly above your shoulders.  Your arms should be fully extended and form a 90 degree angle from your torso and the floor.
The palms of your hands should be facing forward, and your elbows should be tucked in.  This will be your starting position.
Now, inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells until they are near your ears.  Be sure to keep your upper arms stationary and your elbows tucked in.
Then, exhale and use your triceps to return the weight to the starting position.

Grab a couple of dumbbells and adopt a standing position with your feet shoulder width apart. Have your arms in front of you but still perpendicular to the floor (palms facing your thighs). Slightly bend at your knees and maintain a straight back throughout. This will be your starting position.

Whilst keeping your knees stationary, inhale as you lean forwards from your waist (remember to keep a straight back), keeping the dumbbells close to you as you go. Keep lowering the dumbbells towards your feet until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings.

After a brief pause bring your torso straight again by pushing your hips forward. Tip: Remember, this whole movement is performed with 'stiff' legs (slight bend at the knee, but no more) and a straight back throughout. Exhale as you perform this part of the movement.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: This is not an exercise for those with back issues. Avoid jerking motions as this could cause serious injury.
Variation: Barbell Stiff Legged Deadlift.

Grab a dumbbell in each hand and stand with a shoulder width stance in front of a step. The height of the step should be realistic to the ability of the user (palms facing each other). This is your starting position.

Place your right foot on the step/bench. Step up onto the step/bench by pushing through your right heel. As your do so your hip and knee joints should straighten out. Lift your left leg onto the step/bench at this point. Exhale as you perform this part of the movement.

Step off the step/bench with the left leg by bending at the hip and knee joints of the right leg, inhale as you do so. Return to the original standing position by taking your right foot off the step/bench.
Alternate as you go along and repeat until you have performed the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Barbell Step Ups or Single Leg Leg Press.

Position a bench behind you as if you were going to sit down. Now, grab two dumbbells and stand with a shoulder width stance, toes pointing slightly outward (palms facing the side of your legs), infront of the bench.
Throughout the movement you should keep your head up and maintain a stright back. This will be your starting position.

Descend slowly by bending your knees until your thighs are parallel with the floor and your glutes touch the bench. Keep a straight back so that your posture is not compromised. Tip: When at the bottom of the movement your knees should be behind your toes.

Once you have hit the bottom of the movement, pause for a second before pushing your torso back into the starting position through your heels. As you descend inhale and as you ascend, exhale. You should now be at the starting position, ready to go again.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Always be aware of your posture with this exercise as if performed incorrectly, could cause injury.
Variations: Barbell Squats or Smith Machine Squats.
Grab two dumbbells and stand with a shoulder width stance, toes pointing slightly outward (palms facing the side of your legs).
Throughout the movement you should keep your head up and maintain a straight back. This will be your starting position.

Descend slowly by bending your knees until your thighs are parallel with the floor. Keep a straight back so that your posture is not compromised. Tip: When at the bottom of the movement your knees should be behind your toes.

Once you have hit the bottom of the movement, pause for a second before pushing your torso back into the starting position through your heels. As you descend inhale and as you ascend, exhale. You should now be at the starting position, ready to go again.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Always be aware of your posture with this exercise as if performed incorrectly, could cause injury.
Variations: Barbell Squats or Smith Machine Squats.

Grab two dumbbells and lay flat on a bench with the dumbbells pressed straight up towards the ceiling. Adopt a pronated grip (palms facing away from you), with the ends of the dumbbells gently touching one another and your elbows tucked in. This will be your starting position.

Inhale as you descend the dumbbells down towards your forehead. Be sure to keep your upper arms stationary and your elbows tucked in. When you get to within an inch of your forehead pause for a second.
Then whilst exhaling, use your triceps to return the dumbbells along the same path to the starting position. Your arms should be fully extended once again and pointing towards the ceiling.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: EZ Bar Skull Crushers or Tricep Rope Extensions.

Grab a dumbbell in your right hand with a neutral grip and adopt a shoulder width stance. Allow your arm to hang perpendicular to the floor by your side. Place your left hand across your body to reduce influence. This is your starting position.

To initiate the movement, inhale and lean laterally towards your right side, descending the dumbbell towards your knee. Continue to descend until either you cannot lean any further or the dumbbell is next to your knee.
After a brief pause, exhale and flex the obliques and intercostal muscles on your left side and return your torso along the same path back to its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variations: Oblique Crunch or Kneeling Oblique Cable Crunch.
Grab a dumbbell in each hand and stand with a shoulder width stance. Your arms should be fully extended by your sides, your palms facing each other. This will be your starting position.

As you exhale, lift the dumbbells by lifting your shoulders and squeezing them in towards your ears. Hold this position for a second. Tip: This movement is generated through the lifting motion created from your shoulders. Therefore your arms should remain by your sides throughout and relaxed for the most part.
After this pause, return the dumbbells back to the starting position through the descent of your shoulders.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Barbell Shrugs or Smith Machine Shrugs.

Grab a dumbbell in each hand and adopt a shoulder width stance. Without leaning backward or putting your posture into jeopardy, press the dumbbells above your head so that you arms are fully extended, yet slightly flexed/bent at your elbows. Make sure your palms are facing forward at this point. This will be your starting position.

Slowly and in a controlled fashion, descend the dumbbells until they reach the side of your head (parallel with your ears), creating a 90 degree angle at your elbows. Inhale on the downward motion and flare your elbows out wide.
After a brief pause, exhale and press the dumbbells back along this path to their starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Seated Dumbbell Shoulder Press or Cable Shoulder Press.

name_en: Single Leg Dumbbell Seated Calf Raises
instructions_en: Grab a dumbbell and take a seat on a flat bench. Have your feet flat on the floor with your knees and ankles at slightly an acute angle (under 90 degrees) and the dumbbell placed on your left thigh. Tip: The further down your thigh towards your knee you place the dumbbell, the harder the exercise. Keep hold of the dumbbell for stability as you perform this exercise.

To initiate this movement, exhale and lift your left heel off the floor by pressing the ball of your foot into the ground. This will engage and contract your calf muscle. Continue to contract your calf until it is at peak contraction.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your heel along the same path back to its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variations: Seated Calf Raises or Smith Machine Calf Raises.

Grab two dumbbells and take a seat on a flat bench. Have your feet flat on the floor with your knees and ankles at slightly an acute angle (under 90 degrees) and the dumbbells placed on your thighs. Tip: The further down your thighs towards your knees you place the dumbbells, the harder the exercise. Keep hold of the dumbbells for stability as you perform this exercise.

To initiate this movement, exhale and lift your heels off the floor by pressing the balls of your feet into the ground. This will engage and contract your calf muscles. Continue to contract your calves until they are at peak contraction.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your heels along the same path back to their starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Seated Calf Raises or Smith Machine Calf Raises.

Grab two dumbbells and stand with a shoulder width stance, toes pointing slightly outward (palms facing the side of your legs) in front of a flat bench. Have a step set up around a 12 inches in front of you.
Throughout the movement you should keep your head up and maintain a straight back. This will be your starting position.

Descend slowly by bending your knees until your thighs are parallel with the floor and your glutes are gently touching the bench. Keep a straight back so that your posture is not compromised. Tip: When at the bottom of the movement your knees should be behind your toes.

Once you have hit the bottom of the movement, pause for a second before aggressively pressing through your thighs and extending at your ankle, knee and hip joints so accelerate your body into a jump onto the step in front of you. Land softly by bending at your knee and hip joints, before standing upright into full extension.
After a brief pause, step back off of the step and into your starting position once more.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Box Jumps or Dumbbell Jump Squats.

Grab two dumbbells with an underhand grip (palms facing forward) and stand with a shoulder width stance. Make sure you are standing upright with a slight bend at your knees and elbows. Let your arms hang infront of you, perpendicular to the floor. This will be your starting position.
Whilst exhaling and maintaining a stationary torso, lift the dumbbells out to your side, taking them as wide as you can. Continue to lift until your arms are parallel to the floor.
Pause at the top of the movement for a split second.
As you inhale, lower the dumbbells along the same path back to their starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

Grab two dumbbells and place them next a flat bench. With your right knee, kneel on one end of the bench with your left leg remaining straight and on the floor for support. Lean on the bench with your right hand whilst taking hold of the dumbbell with your left hand. Your torso at this point should be parallel with the floor with a neutral spine. This will be your starting position.

Keeping your upper arm close to your body, pull the dumbbell up to the side of your hip whilst maintaining a stationary torso. Exhale as you lift the dumbbell. Tip: Contract your back muscles in order to create this movement. Your arms should remain as passive as possible.
Lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position. Inhale as you perform this step.
Repeat the movement for the recommended amount of repetitions and then switch sides.
Variations: Seated Cable Row or Alternating Kettlebell Row.
Whilst in a standing position, grab two dumbbells and keep your arms by your sides. This will be your starting position.

Take a step backwards (slightly larger than a normal step) and as you land, drop your body down so that you create two right angles at your knees. Inhale as you do so whilst maintaining a straight torso.
Make sure that you keep your front knee over your hell and not over your toes. Your front shin should now be perpendicular to the floor.
As you exhale, use mainly the heel of your front foot and push off the floor towards the starting position again.
Alternate legs as you go and repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Walking Lunges or Alternating Barbell Lunges.

Grab a dumbbell in your left hand and lay face down on an incline bench (around 30-45 degrees) and allow your left arm to hang perpendicular to the floor. Have your head off the end of the bench so that only your torso is supported with your right arm, placed behind your back. This will be your starting position.
Inhale and pull the back of your left hand towards the ceiling.
Once the dumbbell is horizontal to the floor, pause for a brief second, exhale and slowly return the dumbbell along the same path, back into its starting position.
Repeat until you have completed the prescribed amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variations: Rear Delt Machine Flyes or Cable Rear Delt Fly.
Grab a couple of dumbbells and adopt a shoulder width stance. Allow your arms to hang perpendicular to the floor with a neutral grip (palms facing your thighs). This will be your starting position.

Whilst exhaling, contract your shoulders to elevate the dumbbells towards your arm pits. Your elbows should flare out wide as you go and always stay above the dumbbells. Continue until your shoulders are at peak contraction.
Pause for a brief moment, Inhale and lower the dumbbells along the same path towards the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Upright Barbell Rows or Upright Cable Rows.

Grab a couple of dumbbells and lay face down on an incline bench (around 30-45 degrees) and allow your arms to hang perpendicular to the floor with an underhand grip (palms facing forward). Have your head off the end of the bench so that only your torso is supported. This will be your starting position.
Whilst exhaling, contract your biceps and curl the dumbbells up to shoulder height. Hold the contraction for a second as you squeeze your biceps. Tip: Only the forearms should move.
Inhale as you lower the dumbbells along the same path towards the starting position.
Continue for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Barbell Curls or Cable Curls.
Grab a dumbbell in each hand and adopt a shoulder width stance with your arms hanging perpendicular to the floor, by your sides.This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, bend at your knees and hips (more so with your hips than knees) descending into a squat position whilst maintaining a straight back. Your chest, shoulders and head should be facing forward at this point.
Next, drive through your heels and quads and start elevating the dumbbells towards your shoulders.
Next you need to drive your pelvis forward when the dumbbells get to your mid thigh. This coupled with the extension through your knee and ankle joints, will propel the dumbbells upward.
Once you have thrusted your hips forward, it is time to shrug as hard as you can in order to lift the dumbbells further.

When at full extension, aggressively squat under the dumbbells so that you can take the load on your front deltoids (shoulders) in a front squat position. Continue to squat until you are comfortable to drive upward through your heels, quads and glutes.
Keep driving upward until you are in a standing position.
Whilst maintaining good posture, bring the dumbbells down to their starting position, reset, and go again!
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Barbell Cleans or Kettlebell Cleans.

Grab a dumbbell with an overhand grip and adopt a standing position allowing your arm to hang perpendicular to the floor. This will be your starting position. Tip: Maintain a straight back and keep the dumbbell as close to your body as possible throughout the movement.

Exhale and pull the dumbbell up towards your chin by contracting your shoulders and flaring your elbow out wide. Continue to pull the dumbbell upward until you reach your chin. At this point your elbow should be above the weight.
Pause for a brief moment, then inhale and return the dumbbell along the same path towards the starting position.
Variations: Upright Barbell Rows or Smith Machine One-Arm Upright Row.

Grab a dumbbell in your left hand and kneel on the end of a bench with your right knee. Place your left leg out wide on the floor for support. Lean on the bench with your right hand keeping your torso parallel to the floor with a neutral spine. Bend your left elbow and pull it in close to your torso so that your forearm is hanging perpendicular to the floor. This will be your starting position.

Whilst keeping your upper arm stationary, use your tricep to lift the dumbbell on an arcing motion behind you. Exhale as you do so and continue to lift the dumbbell until your forearm is parallel to the floor and your elbow is fully extended. Tip: Only your forearm should move.
After a brief pause, slowly lower the dumbbell along the same path back into its starting position as you inhale.
Repeat the movement for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variations: Tricep Extension (Rope) or Cable Skull Crushers.

name_en: Single Arm Standing Dumbbell Shoulder Press
instructions_en: Grab a dumbbell in each hand and adopt a shoulder width stance. Without leaning backward or putting your posture into jeopardy, press one of the dumbbells above your head so that you arm is fully extended, yet slightly flexed/bent at your elbow. Make sure your palm is facing forward at this point. Allow your other arm to hang perpendicular to the floor by your side. This will be your starting position.

Slowly and in a controlled fashion, descend the dumbbell until it reaches the side of your head (parallel with your ear), creating a 90 degree angle at your elbow. Inhale throughout this motion and flare your elbow out wide.
After a brief pause, exhale and press the dumbbell back along this path to its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Alternating Kettlebell Press or Arnold Press.

Grab a dumbbell in your left hand and lay face down on an incline bench (around 30-45 degrees) and allow your left arm to hang perpendicular to the floor. Have your head off the end of the bench so that only your torso is supported with your right arm, placed behind your back. This will be your starting position.
Inhale and pull the back of your left hand towards the ceiling.
Once the dumbbell is horizontal to the floor, pause for a brief second, exhale and slowly return the dumbbell along the same path, back into its starting position.
Repeat until you have completed the prescribed amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variations: Face Pulls or Cable Rear Delt Fly.

Grab a dumbbell in your right hand and lay on a bench (on your right side). Lay with your left arm across your body for stability. Hold the dumbbell with your palm facing your right shoulder. This is your starting position.
Exhale and rotate your forearm so that your palm is facing away from you.
After a brief pause, inhale and slowly return the dumbbell along the same path, back into its starting position.
Repeat until you have completed the prescribed amount of repetitions and swap sides.

name_en: Dumbbell Lying One-Arm Rear Lateral Raise
instructions_en: Grab a dumbbell in your left hand and lay on a bench (on your right side). Lay with your left arm across your body, allowing the dumbbell to lay next to your right shoulder, palm facing your torso. This is your starting position.
Inhale and pull the back of your left hand towards the wall behind you, therefore opening up your torso.
Once the dumbbell is vertical from the floor, pause for a brief second, exhale and slowly return the dumbbell along the same path, back into its starting position.
Repeat until you have completed the prescribed amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variations: Face Pulls or Rear Delt Machine Flyes.
Whilst in a standing position, grab two dumbbells and keep your arms by your sides. This will be your starting position.

Take a step forward (slightly larger than a normal step) and as you land, drop your body down so that you create two right angles at your knees. Inhale as you do so whilst maintaining a straight torso.
Make sure that you keep your front knee over your heel and not over your toes. Your front shin should now be perpendicular to the floor.
As you exhale, use mainly the heel of your front foot and push off the floor towards the starting position again.
Alternate legs as you go and repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Walking Lunges or Alternating Barbell Lunges.

Grab two dumbbells with a neutral grip and stand with a shoulder width stance. Make sure you are standing upright with a slight bend at your knees and elbows. Let your arms hang infront of you, perpendicular to the floor. This will be your starting position.
Whilst exhaling and maintaining a stationary torso, lift the dumbbells out to your side, taking them as wide as you can. Continue to lift until your arms are parallel to the floor.
Pause at the top of the movement for a split second.
As you inhale, lower the dumbbells to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Seated Lateral Raises or Cable Lateral Raises.

Grab a dumbbell in each hand and adopt a shoulder width stance. Whilst maintaining a straight back, you should lean forward from your hips until your torso is almost parallel with the floor. Have your knees slightly bent and your elbows pinned in to the sides of your body. Your elbows should be at 90 degrees at this point. This is your starting position.

Whilst keeping your upper arms stationary, use your triceps to lift the dumbbells on an arcing motion behind you. Exhale as you do so and continue to lift the dumbbells until your forearms are parallel to the floor and your elbows are fully extended. Tip: Only your forearms should move.
After a brief pause, slowly lower the dumbbells along the same path back into their starting position as you inhale.
Repeat the movement for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Overhead Rope Tricep Extensions or Narrow Grip Barbell Bench Press.

Grab a dumbbell in each hand and lay down on an incline bench (around 30 degrees), bringing the dumbbells beside your chest. Keep your elbows at a 90-degree angle and your forearms perpendicular to the floor. Once here, keep your palms facing away from you, exhale and press the dumbbells towards the ceiling.
Keeping your arms fully extended towards the ceiling, exhale and press your shoulders towards the ceiling too, allowing your shoulder blades to separate.
After a brief pause, inhale and slowly return the dumbbells along the same path, back into their starting position.
Repeat until you have completed the prescribed amount of repetitions.
Variations: Barbell Incline Shoulder Raise or Machine Shoulder (Military) Press.

Grab a dumbbell in each hand and take a seat on a flat bench. Lay down and bring the weights by the side of your chest. Press the dumbbells towards the ceiling with a neutral grip (palms facing each other) until you have nearly locked out your elbows, a slight bend should be present to preserve your joints. This will be your starting position.
Maintaining this slight flex at your elbows, lower your arms in a wide arcing motion until you feel your chest stretching, inhale as you do so.
Following the same path as you went down, squeeze your pectoral muscles and bring your arms back to the starting position, remembering to exhale as you go.
Pause for a second and then repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Flat Bench Cable Flyes or Pec Deck.

Grab a dumbbell in each hand and lay on your back. Have your elbows bent at a 90 degree angle, flared out wide with your forearms vertical from the floor. Your palms should be facing your lower body. This will be your starting position.
Initiate the movement by exhaling and pressing the dumbbells towards the ceiling until your elbow joint is nearly at full extension.
After a brief pause, inhale and slowly return the dumbbells along the same path, back into their starting position.
Repeat until you have completed the prescribed amount of repetitions.
Variations: Barbell Bench Press or Chest Press.
Grab a dumbbell in each hand and adopt a shoulder width stance with your arms hanging perpendicular to the floor, by your sides.This will be your starting position.
To initiate the movement, exhale and curl the dumbbells both up and outward whilst rotating your palms to face the ceiling.
Continue on an upward motion until your biceps are optimally contracted.
After this, inhale and return the dumbbells along the same path back to their starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Standing Concentration Curls or Concentration Curls.
Grab a dumbbell in each hand and adopt a shoulder width stance with your arms hanging perpendicular to the floor, by your sides.This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, bend at your knees and hips (more so with your hips than knees) descending into a squat position whilst maintaining a straight back. Your chest, shoulders and head should be facing forward at this point.
Next, drive through your heels and quads and start elevating the dumbbells towards your shoulders.
Next you need to drive your pelvis forward when the dumbbells get to your mid thigh. This coupled with the extension through your knee and ankle joints, will propel the dumbbells upward.
Once you have thrusted your hips forward, it is time to shrug as hard as you can in order to lift the dumbbells further.

When at full extension, aggressively squat under the dumbbells so that you can take the load on your front deltoids (shoulders) in a front squat position. Continue to squat until you are comfortable to drive upward through your heels, quads and glutes.
Keep driving upward until you are in a standing position.
Whilst maintaining good posture, bring the dumbbells down to their starting position, reset, and go again!
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Barbell Cleans or Kettlebell Cleans.
Grab a dumbbell in each hand, in a standing position, with your arms at full length, palms facing away from you. This will be your starting position.
Whilst exhaling, contract your biceps and bring the dumbbells up to shoulder height. Hold the contraction for a second as you squeeze your biceps. Tip: Only the forearms should move.
Inhale as you lower the dumbbells toward the starting position.
Continue for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Seated Rotating Bicep Curls or Standing Barbell Curls.

Grab a dumbbell in each hand and lay down on a bench, bringing the dumbbells beside your chest. Keep your elbows at a 90-degree angle and your forearms perpendicular to the floor. Once here, keep your palms facing away from you, exhale and press the dumbbells towards the ceiling.
Squeeze your pectoral muscles when your arms are at full extension. Once you have completed this, inhale and bring your arms back down beside your chest until your elbows are at 90-degrees once more.
Repeat the movement for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Always have a spotter with you to help you throughout the movement and to help unload the dumbbells after completion.
Variations: Neutral Grip Dumbbell Chest Press or Barbell Bench Press.

Grab a dumbbell in each hand and take a seat on a decline bench. Lay down and bring the weights by the side of your chest. Press the dumbbells towards the ceiling with a neutral grip (palms facing each other) until you have nearly locked out your elbows, a slight bend should be present to preserve your joints. This will be your starting position.

Maintaining this slight flex at your elbows, inhale and bend at your elbows creating an arcing motion with the dumbbells so that your hands are heading towards your forehead. The weight should be stretching your triceps. Remember to keep your elbows as close together as possible and that your upper arms shouldn't move throughout.
After a brief pause, exhale and return the dumbbells and your hands along the same path to the starting position.
Pause for a second and then repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Decline EZ Bar Triceps Extension or Bench Dips.

Grab a dumbbell in each hand and take a seat on a decline bench. Lay down and bring the weights by the side of your chest. Press the dumbbells towards the ceiling with a neutral grip (palms facing each other) until you have nearly locked out your elbows, a slight bend should be present to preserve your joints. This will be your starting position.
Maintaining this slight flex at your elbows, lower your arms in a wide arcing motion until you feel your chest stretching, inhale as you do so.
Following the same path as you went down, squeeze your pectoral muscles and bring your arms back to the starting position, remembering to exhale as you go.
Pause for a second and then repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Flat Bench Cable Flyes or Pec Deck.

Grab a dumbbell in each hand and secure your legs on a decline bench. Once you have done this, lay down on the decline bench bringing the dumbbells to the side of your chest at shoulders width. Have your palms facing away from you. This will be your starting position.

Whilst breathing out, press the dumbbells towards the ceiling using your pectoral muscles until your elbows are fully extended. Tip: Throughout the motion, the forearms should always be perpendicular to the floor.
Squeeze your chest at the top of the movement for a second and then descend the dumbbells back into their starting position, whilst inhaling.
Repeat the movement for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: When you have completed your set, have a spotter help you with your dumbbells.
Variations: Decline Barbell Press.
Grab a dumbbell in each hand and adopt a standing position with your arms relaxed by your sides. This will be your starting position.
To initiate this movement you will need to elevate your elbows so that they are at shoulder height (allow your forearms to hang perpendicular to the floor).

Once you have done this, externally rotate your shoulder joints 180 degrees, to bring your forearms upright from the floor. This will maintain the 90 degree bend at your elbows but instead of your palms facing behind you and aiming at the floor, they will be facing forward and aiming at the ceiling.
Next, extend your arms upward to press the dumbbells towards the ceiling, bringing them together at the top of the movement.
After a brief pause, you should reverse this movement and return the dumbbells along the same path, back into their starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Arnold Dumbbell Press or Smith Machine Overhead Shoulder Press.
Grab a dumbbell in each hand and adopt a standing position with your elbows turned outwards and palms facing your thighs. This will be your starting position.

Whilst maintaining a stationary upper arm, bend at your right elbow to bring the dumbbell up and across your body towards your left shoulder. Exhale as you do so and contract your bicep to create the movement.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the dumbbell to the starting position along the same path.
Repeat the movement with your left side.
Continue alternating for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Alternate Hammer Curls or Cable Curls.

Stand on the reverse side of an incline bench as if you were spotting someone. Grab a dumbbell in one hand and rest your elbow on the headrest of the bench. Have a ‘palms up’ grip and position yourself so that your arm is running down the length of the bench. Position your non-lifting hand on the incline bench for stability. Your chest should be pressed up against the top part of the incline bench and you should adopt a wide stance with your feet. This will be your starting position.

While holding the upper arm stationary, contract the bicep and curl the dumbbell upward as you exhale. Only your forearm should move. Continue the movement until your bicep is fully contracted and the dumbbell is at shoulder level. Hold this position for a second and as you inhale, slowly reverse the movement until you are in the starting position once again.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Low Cable Bicep Curl.

name_en: Close-Grip Push-Up off of a Dumbbell
instructions_en: Grab a heavy dumbbell (one that will not rock or fall over) and place it upright on the floor. Next, kneel down on the floor in front of the dumbbell and place your hands on the edges of it. Lean forward so that you are now weight baring on your arms. Now you should come up onto your feet so that your body is suspended above the floor, the only points of contact (with the floor) should be the dumbbell (with your hands on it) and feet. Your body should be in one straight line from head to toe and your arms perpendicular to the floor under your chest. This will be your starting position.
Inhale and slowly lower your chest towards the dumbbell whilst keeping your elbows close to your body.

Once your chest is around an inch off the dumbbell, pause for a brief movement, then exhale and press through your palms to return your body along the same path to the starting position. Tip: Squeeze your triceps as you do so and when you get to the top of the movement, do not lock your elbows out.
Repeat the movement for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Close-Grip Barbell Bench Press or Smith Machine Close-Grip Bench Press.
Grab a dumbbell in each hand and lay on a flat bench. Have your hands by the side of your chest with a neutral grip. This will be your starting position.
Ensuring that your elbows remain as close to your body whilst remaining parallel to each other throughout the movement, press both dumbbells towards the ceiling in a controlled manner.
Squeeze your triceps at the top of the contraction for a second before reversing the movement back into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Narrow Grip Barbell Bench Press.

Grab a dumbbell in each hand and lay face down on an incline bench (around 30-45 degrees) and allow your arms to hang perpendicular to the floor. Have your head off the end of the bench so that only your torso is supported. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and pull the dumbbells up to the side of your hips whilst keeping your torso in contact with the bench. Keep your upper arms close to your body as you go to activate your lats as much as possible. Tip: Contract your back muscles in order to create this movement. Your arms should remain as passive as possible.
Lower the dumbbells back to the starting position, inhaling as you go.
Repeat the movement for the recommended amount of repetitions and then switch sides.
Variations: One Arm Dumbbell Row or Seated One-Arm Cable Pulley Row.
Grab a dumbbell in each hand and lean into an incline bench, making sure your head is off the end.
Using a neutral grip, allow your arms to hang perpendicular to the floor. This will be your starting position.
Pull your shoulder blades together and bend at your elbows in order to row the dumbbells to your side. Try to keep your elbows as close to your body as possible throughout the movement.
Pause at the contracted position, and return to the starting position in a controlled manner.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

Grab a weight plate and adopt a shoulder width, standing stance. Next, make sure that you grip the plate in a '9' and '3' O'Clock position. Now, extend your arms so that they are in front of your body at shoulder height (elbows slightly bent). This will be your starting position.
To initiate the movement, twist the plate clockwise until you can twist no more. Pretend you were turning a steering wheel whilst maintaining shoulder height throughout.
After a brief pause, reverse the movement and twist the weight plate anti-clockwise until you can twist no more.
Repeat these twists until you have completed the prescribed amount of repetitions.
Variations: Arnold Press or Smith Machine Military Press.

name_en: Dumbbell Bent Over Rows Neutral Grip
instructions_en: With a dumbbell in each hand (palms facing each other), bend your knees slightly and bring your torso forward by bending at your hips; as you bend make sure to keep your back straight until it is almost parallel to the floor. Tip: Make sure that you keep your spine in a neutral position. The weights should hang directly in front of you as your arms are perpendicular to the floor and your torso. This is your starting position.

While keeping the torso stationary, lift the dumbbells to your side (as you exhale), elevating elbows towards the ceiling (do not exert any force with the forearm other than holding the weights). At the top contracted position, squeeze the back muscles and hold for a second.
Slowly lower the weight to the starting position as you inhale.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution:
This exercise is not recommended for people with back issues. A Low Cable Row is a better choice for people with back pain.
Ensure perfect form and never round the back as this can cause injury.
Be cautious with the weights you are using. If you are in doubt, use a lesser weight.
Variations: Low Cable Row or a Bent Over Barbell Row
